detaileddescription.para: When calling lely_compat_memcpy() with the area, source data pointer Then count bytes from memory area is overwritten with new data; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memcpy() for the area with zero count
Then memory area is not modified; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memmove() with the area, source data pointer and data count
Then count bytes from memory area is overwritten with new data; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memmove() with the area with zero count
Then memory area is not modified; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memmove() with source pointer value larger than target pointer value
Then count bytes in memory area is overwritten; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memmove() with source pointer value smaller Then count bytes in memory area is overwritten; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memmove() with source and target poiner equal
Then memory is left unmodified; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memcmp() with zero byte count
Then 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_memcmp() with 'greater' area as the first argument
Then value greater than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_memcmp() with 'greater' area as the second argument
Then value less than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_memcmp() with equal areas
Then 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strcmp() with 'greater' string as the first argument
Then value greater than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strcmp() with 'greater' string as the second argument
Then value less than 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strcmp() with equal strings

detaileddescription.para: Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strcmp() with shorter string as the first argument

detaileddescription.para: Then value less than 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strcmp() with shorter string as the second argument

detaileddescription.para: Then value greater than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with zero byte count
Then 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with 'greater' string as the first argument
Then value greater than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with 'greater' string as the second argument
Then value less than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with strings equal on the first count of bytes
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with equal strings

detaileddescription.para: Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with shorter string as the first argument

detaileddescription.para: Then value less than 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: When calling lely_compat_strncmp() with shorter string as the second argument

detaileddescription.para: Then value greater than 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_memset() for the area with pattern and size
Then pattern is set for specifed count of bytes; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_memset() for the area with pattern and zero size
Then memory area is not modified; memory area address is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string
Then 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string
Then string length is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string and larger maximum length
Then 0 is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string and larger maximum length
Then string length is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string and smaller maximum length
Then the maximum length is returned
When calling lely_compat_strlen() with the string and zero maximum length
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.
